raw_consumer_statement:
American Express Fraud Department is failing to investigate or take claims of a XXXX XXXX charge that has been attempted on my American Express Gold Card multiple times between XXXX XXXX. 

I expressed that I do not do business with XXXX or XXXX. I expressed that I believe my account was comprised internally because I never gave this card number out to any XXXX or XXXX. 

I expressed further that my research shown that this is a common scam with ONLY American Express users where their accounts are compromised with this charge for small amounts then hit with larger amounts. 

I also expressed the stress I had a year ago with a the charge and the immense amount of work I had to go through to prove this was indeed a fraud charge. 

American Express Fraud Department failed to address this concern. Failed to solve the underlying issues, and stated their solution was to place a merchant block. 

The chat Fraud Department claimed that this was due to an old card being charged. They failed to see the underlying cause. 


Now I had to beg American Express Fraud Department to issue a new account number to prevent this fraud. As I reiterated that I never gave out this card to XXXX or XXXX. I believe my account was internationally compromised to allow an attempted charge to happen. 

This has caused XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX 

I know I am not the only American Express member out there dealing with this issue. XXXX users and other forums have expressed that they are experiencing this too.
